description: Create a plan-view drawing of a T-intersection in LibreCAD centered at the drawing origin (0, 0). The Main Road runs East-West with a length of 50m (x=-25 to x=25) and a width of 12m (edges at y=-6 and y=6). The Branch Road runs North from the Main Road with a width of 8m (edges at x=-4 and x=4) and extends 25m North from the Main Road's north edge (y=6 to y=31). Include two 8m radius curb return arcs connecting the Branch Road to the Main Road at the NW and NE corners. Draw center lines for both roads along y=0 and x=0. Organize the drawing using layers: ROAD_EDGE for straight boundaries, CURB_RETURN for arcs, CENTER_LINE for center lines, and ANNOTATIONS for text and dimensions. On the ANNOTATIONS layer, include text labels "MAIN ROAD" and "BRANCH ROAD" and dimension lines showing the widths of both roads (12m and 8m). Save the DXF file to /home/ga/Documents/LibreCAD/t_intersection.dxf.
